title: Platanthera citrina
intro: Platanthera citrina, commonly known as the golden orchid or yellow bog orchid, is a species of orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Platanthera citrina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoid glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Platanthera citrina include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to relieve coughs and congestion.
taxonomy: Platanthera citrina is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. It belongs to the genus Platanthera, which consists of terrestrial orchids. Within the genus, Platanthera citrina is further classified as a species of yellow-spotted orchid. The species is native to the mountainous regions of the eastern United States, where it thrives in moist, wooded areas. Its classification as a terrestrial orchid highlights its unique adaptations to the soil environment.
